name: reply
title: Discord Reply
description: Reply on the messaging channel. Pass chat_id from the inbound message. Optionally pass reply_to, files, embeds, and components (buttons, selects, etc).

chat_id.description: Channel ID where the message will be sent

reply_to.description: Message ID to thread under. Use message_id from the inbound <channel> block, or an id from fetch.

files.description: Absolute file paths to attach (images, logs, etc). Max 10 files, 25MB each.

embeds.description: Discord embed objects. Fields: title, description, color (int), fields [{name, value, inline}], footer {text}, timestamp.

components.description: Discord message components. Use Action Rows containing Buttons, Select Menus, etc. See Discord Components V2 docs.

name: react
title: Reaction
description: Add an emoji reaction to a message. Unicode emoji work directly; custom emoji need the <:name:id> form.

message_id.description: ID of the message to react to

emoji.description: Unicode emoji (e.g. "👍") or custom emoji in <:name:id> format

name: edit_message
title: Edit Message
description: Edit a message the bot previously sent. Supports text, embeds, and components.

message_id.description: ID of the bot message to edit

name: download_attachment
title: Download Attachment
description: Download attachments from a message to the local inbox. Use after fetch shows a message has attachments (marked with +Natt). Returns file paths ready to Read.

message_id.description: ID of the message with attachments

name: fetch
title: Fetch
description: Fetch recent messages from a channel. Returns oldest-first with message IDs. The platform's search API isn't exposed to bots, so this is the only way to look back.

channel.description: Channel name label (e.g. "main", "general") as configured in channelsConfig

name: fetch_many
title: Fetch Many
description: Fetch recent messages from multiple channels in one call. Use this when you need to look back across 2+ channels without serial fetches.

channels.description: Channel name labels as configured in channelsConfig

limit.description: Max messages per channel (default 20, capped at 100).

name: schedule_status
title: Schedule Status
description: Show all configured schedules, their next fire time, and whether they are currently running.

name: trigger_schedule
title: Trigger Schedule
description: Manually trigger a named schedule immediately, ignoring time/day constraints.

name: schedule_control
title: Schedule Control
description: Defer or skip a schedule. Use "defer" to suppress for N minutes (default 30), or "skip_today" to suppress for the rest of the day.

minutes.description: Defer duration in minutes (default 30, only for defer action)

name: activate_channel_bridge
title: Activate Channel Bridge
description: Activate or deactivate the channel bridge. When active, inbound messages trigger typing indicators, emoji reactions, and auto-forwarding of transcript output to Discord. When inactive, only direct MCP tool calls (reply, fetch) work.

active.description: true to activate, false to deactivate

name: reload_config
title: Reload Config
description: Reload config from disk and re-register all schedules, webhooks, and event rules without restarting.

name: memory
title: Memory Cycle
description: Run memory operations: cycle2/sleep (promote+dedup), flush, rebuild (requires confirm: "REBUILD MEMORY"), prune (requires confirm: "PRUNE OLD ENTRIES"), cycle1, backfill, status, remember (store fact), forget (archive a root), delete (requires confirm: "DELETE ALL MEMORY" — wipes all entries).

element.description: Content for remember; also accepted as a substring match target for forget

maxDays.description: Age threshold in days for the `prune` action. Unclassified entries older than this are deleted. Default 30, minimum 1. Ignored by other actions.

confirm.description: Required for destructive actions: "DELETE ALL MEMORY" for delete, "PRUNE OLD ENTRIES" for prune, "REBUILD MEMORY" for rebuild. Must match exactly.

name: recall
title: Recall
description: Past context from the memory store. `query`: single rich NL query (default — one internal agent judges multi-angle probes & synthesizes) or array of strings (N independent agents, mechanical merge, no cross-synthesis — only for genuinely unrelated asks). Lead: async (merged answer auto-pushed via channel). Role sessions: sync in-turn. Use `background:true/false` to override. External web → `search`, codebase → `explore`.

query.description: Single rich NL query (default — one internal agent judges multi-angle probes & synthesizes) or array of strings (N independent agents, mechanical merge, no cross-synthesis — only for genuinely unrelated asks).

cwd.description: Optional workspace hint. Absolute path; `~` and forward slashes supported.

background.description: Default: true for Lead (async + channel push), false for role sessions (sync, merged answer returned in-turn). Explicit value wins.

name: explore
title: Explore
description: Internal codebase search. Local filesystem only — not web, not memory. `query`: single rich NL query (default — one internal agent judges glob+grep fan-out & synthesizes) or array of strings (N independent agents, mechanical merge, no cross-synthesis — only for genuinely unrelated asks). `cwd` is authoritative search root (no silent fan-out). Lead: async (merged answer auto-pushed via channel). Role sessions: sync in-turn. Use `background:true/false` to override. Past context → `recall`, external web → `search`.

query.description: Single rich NL query (default — one internal agent judges glob+grep fan-out & synthesizes) or array of strings (N independent agents, mechanical merge, no cross-synthesis — only for genuinely unrelated asks).

cwd.description: Authoritative search root. Absolute path; `~` and forward slashes supported. Omit → launch workspace. Target plugin tree via `cwd: "~/.claude/..."`. No silent fan-out.

background.description: Default: true for Lead (async + channel push), false for role sessions (sync, merged answer returned in-turn). Explicit value wins.

name: search
title: Search
description: External web search, URL scrape, GitHub code/issues/repos. `query`: single rich NL query (default — one internal agent judges multi-angle probes & synthesizes; URL → scrape, `owner/repo` → GitHub) or array of strings (N independent agents, mechanical merge, no cross-synthesis — only for genuinely unrelated asks). Lead: async (merged answer auto-pushed via channel). Role sessions: sync in-turn. Use `background:true/false` to override. Past context → `recall`, codebase → `explore`.

query.description: Single rich NL query (default — one internal agent judges multi-angle probes & synthesizes) or array of strings (N independent agents, mechanical merge, no cross-synthesis — only for genuinely unrelated asks).

background.description: Default: true for Lead (async + channel push), false for role sessions (sync, merged answer returned in-turn). Explicit value wins.

name: create_session
title: Create Session
description: Create external AI session with tool access. Auto-injects context. Use preset: full/readonly/mcp. Use agent: Worker/Reviewer.

provider.description: openai, anthropic, gemini, groq, openrouter, xai, copilot, ollama, lmstudio, local

model.description: Provider-specific model id (e.g. "gpt-5", "claude-opus-4", "gemini-2.5-pro"). Must be valid for the chosen provider.

systemPrompt.description: Optional system prompt prepended to the session. When omitted, the plugin injects the default Pool B/C prefix based on role/preset.

preset.description: Tool permission preset: `full` grants read+write+shell, `readonly` restricts to read-only tools, `mcp` exposes only MCP-bridged tools.

files.description: Optional virtual files seeded into the session's working context (path+content pairs). The agent can read these immediately without fs access.

name: list_sessions
title: List Sessions
description: List active orchestrator sessions.

name: close_session
title: Close Session
description: Close an orchestrator session.

sessionId.description: Id of the session to close (as returned by `create_session` or `list_sessions`). Plants a tombstone and aborts any in-flight work; re-closing the same id is a no-op.

name: list_models
title: List Models
description: List available models from all providers.

name: bridge
title: Bridge to External Model
description: Delegate one turn of work to an external agent by role. Role maps to a preset via user-workflow.json (default bindings: worker→SONNET HIGH, reviewer→OPUS XHIGH, debugger→OPUS XHIGH, tester→SONNET HIGH). Detached by default: returns immediately with jobId + sessionId while the worker continues in the background. Use close_session(sessionId) to stop early. Exactly one of prompt, ref, or file must be provided.

prompt.description: The task instruction for the agent.

role.description: Agent role as defined in user-workflow.json. Default roles: worker, researcher, reviewer, debugger, tester. Users may customize — check user-workflow.json for the actual set.

preset.description: Advanced: explicit preset name (bypass role mapping).

context.description: Extra context appended to the prompt.

file.description: Read prompt from a file path.

name: read
title: Read
description: Read file(s). PREFER ARRAY `path` to read multiple files in ONE call — serial reads waste turns and are the #1 iter waste. Use this AFTER you already have a concrete file candidate (`find_symbol`, `code_graph`, `grep`, `glob`, `list`). `mode`: full (default) | head | tail | count. head/tail read the first/last `n` lines; count returns line/word/byte stats. Big files auto-return a head+tail summary unless `full:true` or offset/limit given — for in-file content search use `grep`, not a full read.

path.description: File path, or array of paths for parallel read.

n.description: Lines for head / tail mode. Default 20.

full.description: Opt out of the big-file head/tail cap. Default false.

name: multi_read
title: Multi Read
description: Read multiple files in ONE call. Use this when 2+ file paths are already known. Each entry supports the same options as `read` (`mode`, `n`, `offset`, `limit`, `full`) and runs in parallel.

n.description: Lines for head / tail mode. Default 20.

full.description: Opt out of the big-file head/tail cap. Default false.

reads.description: List of file reads to execute in parallel.

name: edit
title: Edit
description: Replace text in file(s). PREFER MULTI-EDIT FORM — pass `edits` array to batch N edits in one call; same file applies sequentially, different files run in parallel. Single form (`path` + `old_string` + `new_string`) is for a one-off only; serial single edits waste iters. `replace_all:true` drops the uniqueness check.

old_string.description: Text to find (single-edit form, unique unless replace_all).

replace_all.description: Replace every occurrence instead of requiring unique match.

path.description: Per-edit path. Omit to reuse the top-level path.

edits.description: Multi-edit form: array of edits. Each item may specify its own path; if omitted, falls back to the top-level `path`.

name: multi_edit
title: Multi Edit
description: Apply multiple ordered replacements to ONE file in one call. Use this when the file is already known and you need several edits without serial `edit` turns. All-or-nothing on that file.

edits.description: Ordered replacements for the same file.

name: batch_edit
title: Batch Edit
description: Apply edits across MULTIPLE files in one call. Different files run in parallel; repeated edits within the same file are grouped and executed as `multi_edit`. Use this instead of serial `edit` across several files.

edits.description: Edits across one or more files.

name: edit_lines
title: Edit Lines (line-number based)
description: Replace lines [start_line, end_line] inclusive (1-based) with new_content. Use this when unique-string match in `edit` / `multi_edit` is awkward — large files, repeated substrings, or pure line-replace. Pair with `read` (note line numbers) -> `edit_lines`. mtime drift is enforced like `edit`: must `read` first; concurrent external writes return errorCode 7.

end_line.description: Last line to replace (1-based, inclusive). Must be >= start_line.

new_content.description: Replacement content. Newlines inside are preserved. Set empty string to delete the range.

name: write
title: Write
description: Create or overwrite a file. Prefer `apply_patch` for multi-file or context-heavy edits; use `write` when you are creating a new file or replacing the whole contents intentionally. For multiple whole-file writes in one turn, pass `writes` array.

writes.description: Batch whole-file writes. Each item writes one file. Use this when you need to create/replace several files in one call.

name: write_many
title: Write Many
description: Create or overwrite multiple files in one call. Use this for batch whole-file writes; prefer `apply_patch` for multi-file edits that depend on surrounding context.

writes.description: Batch whole-file writes. Each item writes one file.

name: bash
title: Bash
description: Execute a shell command. DEFAULT = one-shot shell. BATCH RELATED COMMANDS with `&&` (stop on fail) or `;` (always run) in a single call — two separate bash turns for dependent work waste a round-trip. Only opt into persistent shell state when you truly need cwd/env/venv continuity: pass `persistent:true` (bridge sessions) or call `bash_session` directly. Set `run_in_background:true` for long builds/tests/servers, then inspect with `job_status` / `job_wait` / `job_read` / `jobs_list`. Destructive patterns (rm -rf /, force-push, format) are blocked.

merge_stderr.description: Merge stderr into stdout (legacy 2>&1 behaviour). Default false: stderr is surfaced as a separate `[stderr]` block.

run_in_background.description: Run command in the background and return a job id immediately. Use for long builds/tests/servers.

persistent.description: Bridge sessions only: opt into persistent shell state. When true, the bridge routes this `bash` call through `bash_session` and reuses the shell on later `persistent:true` calls.

session_id.description: Bridge sessions only: explicit persistent shell session id to reuse. Prefer `persistent:true` unless you need to target a specific shell.

name: jobs_list
title: List Background Jobs
description: List recent background shell jobs with status, pid, and start time. Use this to discover job ids before `job_wait`, `job_status`, or `job_read`.

name: job_status
title: Background Job Status
description: Get one snapshot for a background shell job: pid, command, output paths, and completion state. Do NOT poll this in a loop — prefer `job_wait` while the job is still running.

job_id.description: Background job id returned by bash with run_in_background:true.

name: job_wait
title: Wait For Background Job
description: Wait for a background shell job to finish and return its latest status/summary in one call. Prefer this over repeated job_status polling.

job_id.description: Background job id returned by bash with run_in_background:true.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum time to wait before returning the current running state. Default 30000.

poll_ms.description: Polling interval while waiting. Default 250 ms.

name: job_read
title: Read Background Job Output
description: Read stdout/stderr from a background shell job using the same line-oriented behavior as read/tail. Prefer `job_wait` first; use `job_read` when you need the actual log text after completion or when the status summary is insufficient.

stream.description: Which stream to read. Default stdout.

name: job_cancel
title: Cancel Background Job
description: Terminate a running background shell job.

name: grep
title: Grep
description: ripgrep content search. PREFER ARRAY `pattern` and/or `glob` — OR-joined in ONE call instead of serial greps (biggest iter saver). For identifier/symbol lookup where you know the name but not the file, prefer `find_symbol` instead of grep. Use `grep` for content confirmation, broader text search, or regex. Output modes: `files_with_matches` (default), `content`, `count`. Use `multiline:true` for patterns spanning lines.

-n.description: Show line numbers (content mode, default true).

name: glob
title: Glob
description: File path search via `rg --files`. PREFER ARRAY `pattern` — OR-joined multi-pattern search in ONE call instead of serial globs. Use `grep` for in-file content search.

path.description: Base dir. Default: cwd. Capped at 100.

head_limit.description: Max file paths to return. Default 100; 0 = unlimited.

offset.description: Skip N file paths before applying head_limit.

name: list
title: List Directory
description: Directory inspection. `mode`: list (default, metadata rows: name/type/size/mtime) | tree (ASCII visualization) | find (filter by name/size/mtime). Use this for quick local shape checks (recent files, candidate directories, size/mtime clues). Use `find` mode to filter by filename pattern within a directory tree; for repository-wide filename pattern search use `glob` instead, and for in-file content search use `grep`. Use `find_symbol` for identifier lookup.

path.description: Root directory. Defaults to cwd. Supports `~` expansion.

depth.description: Recursion depth. list: 1 default, max 10. tree: 3 default, max 6.

sort.description: list mode sort key. Default name.

type.description: Filter by entry type. Default any.

offset.description: Skip N rows/entries before applying head_limit.

min_size.description: find mode: minimum size in bytes (file only).

max_size.description: find mode: maximum size in bytes (file only).

modified_after.description: find mode: ISO 8601 date or relative `Nh`/`Nd` (e.g. `24h`, `7d`).

modified_before.description: find mode: ISO 8601 date or relative `Nh`/`Nd`.

name: diff
title: Unified Diff
description:
Unified diff between two files (or two raw strings). Useful for explaining a change before applying it via `edit` / `multi_edit`. Output is standard `--- from
+++ to
@@ ... @@` format. Pass `from_text:true` and/or `to_text:true` to treat the value as inline text instead of a path.

from.description: Path of the "before" file, or raw text when `from_text:true`.

to.description: Path of the "after" file, or raw text when `to_text:true`.

from_text.description: Treat `from` as inline text. Default false.

to_text.description: Treat `to` as inline text. Default false.

context.description: Context lines around hunks. Default 3, clamp [0, 10].

name: code_graph
title: Code Graph
description: Repository graph / symbol navigation tool. Multi-language common graph for overview/imports/dependents/related/impact/symbols/find_symbol/references/callers. Prefer this over raw grep for imports, dependents, callers, references, or symbol-level impact. In the main session, prefer direct aliases (`find_imports`, `find_dependents`, `find_references`, `find_callers`, `find_symbol`) when the question is that specific; use `code_graph` for broader graph/impact questions.

file.description: Path to the target file. Required for non-overview modes.

language.description: Optional language filter for find_symbol (e.g. javascript, typescript, python).

limit.description: Optional result cap for find_symbol. Default 20, max 50.

name: find_symbol
title: Find Symbol
description: Find a symbol/identifier across the repository and return the best declaration/reference candidates with file:line and nearby code. Prefer this over grep when you know the symbol name but not the file. If a best declaration candidate is returned, read that file directly instead of running another grep on the same identifier.

language.description: Optional language filter (e.g. javascript, typescript, python).

name: find_imports
title: Find Imports
description: What does this file import from others? Returns modules/files that the target file pulls in. Use this instead of `code_graph(mode:"imports")` when you already know the file path. Optimized alias for quicker tool selection in the main session.

name: find_dependents
title: Find Dependents
description: Who imports from this file? Returns files that depend on or pull in the target file. Use this instead of `code_graph(mode:"dependents")` when you already know the file path. Optimized alias for quicker tool selection in the main session.

name: find_references
title: Find References
description: Find references for a symbol across the repository. Use this instead of `code_graph(mode:"references")` when you know the symbol and want the direct tool. Optional `file` can narrow the language/source file, but is no longer required. For non-function symbols (constants, type aliases, variables), this is the correct tool — `find_callers` only matches call-site invocations.

symbol.description: Symbol name to resolve references for.

file.description: Optional file path to narrow the language/source file.

language.description: Optional language filter (e.g. javascript, typescript, python).

name: find_callers
title: Find Callers
description: Find caller files for a symbol across the repository. Use this instead of `code_graph(mode:"callers")` when you know the symbol and want the direct tool. Optional `file` can narrow the language/source file, but is no longer required. Only matches call-site invocations — for non-function symbols (constants, type aliases, variables), prefer `find_references` instead.

symbol.description: Symbol name to resolve callers for.

file.description: Optional file path to narrow the language/source file.

language.description: Optional language filter (e.g. javascript, typescript, python).

name: rename_file_refs
title: Rename File Refs
description: Rename or move a file and rewrite resolvable file references in the moved file and its local importers using the common code graph. Preview by default; set `apply:true` to write through apply_patch atomically.

file.description: Existing file to rename or move.

apply.description: Apply the generated patch to disk. Default false = preview only.

name: rename_symbol_refs
title: Rename Symbol Refs
description: Rename a symbol reference across same-language files using the common code graph and exact token-boundary rewrites. Preview by default; set `apply:true` to write through apply_patch.

file.description: File containing or importing the symbol.

apply.description: Apply the rename to disk. Default false = preview only.

name: sg_search
title: AST-Grep Search
description: Structural (AST-level) code search via ast-grep. Matches syntax shapes, not text — e.g. `function $NAME($$$)` finds every function declaration regardless of whitespace / comments / arg count. Prefer over `grep` for code constructs (class declarations, try-catch blocks, `foo(...).then(...)` chains) rather than literal strings. Args: `pattern`, `path`, optional `lang` (auto-detect from extension; .mjs/.cjs mapped to JavaScript via bundled sgconfig.yml).

pattern.description: ast-grep pattern. Use `$NAME` for single captures, `$$$` for sequences, `$$_` for single anonymous. Example: `function $NAME($$$) { $$$ }`.

path.description: File or directory to search. Absolute or cwd-relative.

lang.description: Language override (typescript|javascript|python|rust|go|c|cpp|java|ruby|php|html|css|json|yaml|...). Omit to let ast-grep auto-detect from the file extension.

globs.description: gitignore-style include / exclude glob(s). Prefix with `!` to exclude. Later globs take precedence over earlier ones.

context.description: Lines of context around each match (equivalent to ripgrep -C). Default 0.

name: sg_rewrite
title: AST-Grep Rewrite
description: Structural rewrite via ast-grep. Match a syntax pattern + substitute rewrite template — safer than text search-and-replace because matches respect the language AST (no string literals / comments / unrelated-scope rebinding). **Dry-run by default**; pass `apply:true` to write. Example: `pattern: "$OBJ.foo($$$)"` + `rewrite: "$OBJ?.foo($$$)"` converts every `x.foo(...)` call site to optional chaining.

pattern.description: ast-grep pattern to match (same syntax as sg_search).

rewrite.description: Rewrite template. Metavariables from the pattern (`$NAME`, `$$$`) are substituted. Empty string deletes the match.

path.description: File or directory to rewrite. Absolute or cwd-relative.

apply.description: Apply the rewrite and write to disk. Default false (dry-run preview only). When true, `confirm: "APPLY REWRITE"` is also required.

confirm.description: Required when apply:true — must equal exactly "APPLY REWRITE". Gates against accidental non-atomic file rewrites.

name: apply_patch
title: Apply Unified Diff
description: Apply a unified-diff patch in ONE turn — inverse of `diff`. Prefer this over repeated `read` → `edit` loops when 2+ files change or the exact edit is already clear. Single/multi-file diffs (git-style `--- a/` / `+++ b/` headers, `a/` `b/` prefixes stripped). Patch context lines self-verify, so it skips the normal read-before-edit round-trip. `/dev/null` → new file creates; file → `/dev/null` deletes. Default atomic (`reject_partial:true`) — any failed hunk rejects whole patch. Use `dry_run:true` to preview changes + first failed hunk without writing. Paths resolve against `base_path` (or cwd), scope-checked like other write tools.

patch.description: Unified diff content. Single-file or multi-file. Supports git-style `a/` / `b/` header prefixes and `/dev/null` for create/delete entries.

base_path.description: Directory to resolve relative paths from the diff headers against. Default: current working directory.

dry_run.description: Preview which files would change without writing. Shows first failed hunk per failing file. Default false.

reject_partial.description: If any file's hunk fails, reject the whole patch and write nothing (atomic). Default true. Set false to apply every successful file even when others failed.

name: bash_session
title: Persistent Bash Session
description: Execute a shell command in a long-lived bash session that preserves state (cwd, env, functions, aliases, sourced virtualenvs) across calls until closed or idle-reaped (5 min). Omit `session_id` on first call — response header `[session: <id>]` gives the id to reuse. Pass `close:true` to terminate. Max 10 concurrent sessions; oldest idle evicted when pool fills. Same safety as `bash` (`rm -rf /` / `git push --force` blocked). Use INSTEAD of `bash` for multi-command state-sharing (e.g. `cd project` then `source .venv/bin/activate` then `pytest`) — one call per command, reusing `session_id`.

command.description: Shell command to execute in the session.

session_id.description: Existing session to reuse. Omit to create a new one; the id is returned in the response header as `[session: <id>]`. Unknown ids are minted (stable resume).

timeout.description: Command timeout in milliseconds. Default 30000, max 600000. On timeout the session is killed (caller should mint a new one). Note: dual-unit behaviour (values ≤ 600 treated as seconds) is deprecated — pass milliseconds only.

close.description: Close the session after this command returns. Default false.
